The mayor deplored the fact that his predecessors had seen fit to solve certain problems by destroying part of the historic city center and then rebuilding it almost entirely, in a pattern he called “dramatic” (the first demolition work took place on ''Place Henri-IV'' in 1970). The heart of Suresnes was thus covered with a shopping mall (Suresnes 2) and, in a more scattered fashion, tall buildings, and low-income housing blocks, “which changed the face of Suresnes, giving it that concrete look. [...] These urban developments exacerbated the industrial, economic, and demographic decline of Suresnes.” The mayor noted the successive departure of industrial and commercial activities from the town, even though Suresnes enjoyed what he considered an exceptional location, on the hillside of Mont Valérien, giving it “a view of the Bois de Boulogne and Paris that is unrivaled in the region.”<ref>{{Harvtxt|Conseil Général des Hauts-de-Seine|2004|pp=122–125}}</ref>
[[File:Parc du château Suresnes 17.jpg|thumb|The park of the Château, which was that of the Château de Suresnes, a building since destroyed.]] Christian Dupuy therefore embarked on a vast policy of revitalization and rehabilitation. In his mind, the aim was “to breathe new life into Suresnes, to maintain and reinforce its modern, urban, Parisian, village and provincial character.” To this end, an action plan has been launched to attract new businesses and encourage the creation of small and medium-sized craft industries. To prevent further urban destruction, as in the previous decade, the city was given an architectural and urban heritage protection zone, covering two-thirds of its surface area. Transformed into an ''[[Aire de mise en valeur de l'architecture et du paysage]]'' (architectural and heritage enhancement area) by city council deliberation on February 12, 2014, this public utility easement automatically became a site ''patrimonial remarquable'' (outstanding heritage site) on the date of promulgation of the law on the freedom of creation, architecture, and heritage (2016).<ref>{{Cite web|title=Réglementation d'urbanisme|language=fr|trans-title=Urban planning regulations|url=https://www.suresnes.fr/mon-quotidien/urbanisme-et-environnement/reglementation-durbanisme/|website=suresnes.fr|access-date=November 19, 2018}}</ref> The mayor increases the number of schools, stadiums, gymnasiums, retirement homes, and medical centers, and initiates the creation of a new media library in the early 2000s. Whereas in 1983, the city had just one crèche for every 35,000 inhabitants, by 2004 it had eleven for every 40,000. The cité-jardin was listed as a historic site and rehabilitated between 1985 and 1996. The République district was rebuilt around the former Château de Suresnes, or Château de Bel-Air, and the industrial wastelands of lower Suresnes were reconverted. In the early 2000s, new projects were launched, such as the reconstruction of the Caron-Jaurès market in the cité-jardin. The number of businesses increased, contributing half of the town's tax revenues. Finally, between 1983 and 1993, the surface area of public green spaces doubled.<ref name=":11">{{Harvtxt|Conseil Général des Hauts-de-Seine|2004|pp=125–127}}</ref> Under his first term of office, the renovation of the town center was completed (with the ''Esplanade des Courtieux''),<ref name=":36" /> environmental management was taken into consideration, obsolete equipment was replaced and low-income housing estates were rehabilitated. The oldest school in Suresnes, École Jules-Ferry, was completely renovated in 1986. In September 1987, the Henri-Dunant children's village was created and, in February 1988, the République Nursery School. In June 1988, the ''Parc du Château'', heir to the garden of the former Château de Suresnes, demolished shortly before to make way for a housing development, was inaugurated.<ref>{{Harvtxt|Prévost|1989|pp=200–208}}</ref> [[File:Usine Philips Suresnes 41-43 rue de Verdun.jpg|left|thumb|Philips Company, ''33 Rue de Verdun'', one of the many companies headquartered in Suresnes.]] In 1989, Suresnes had 3,500 sports enthusiasts in 46 associations, covering 29 disciplines (basketball, boules, horse riding, soccer, karate, gymnastics, etc.).<ref>{{Harvtxt|Prévost|1989|p=230}}</ref> Many sportsmen and women hail from the town, including Philippe Legrand, three-time French 10,000-meter champion, and [[Gérard Brunel]], French 400-meter hurdles champion.<ref name=":58">{{Harvtxt|Prévost|1989|pp=234–235}}</ref> The town also boasts a municipal music, dance, and drama conservatory, which in 1989 welcomed over 1,000 students in 24 disciplines.<ref>{{Harvtxt|Prévost|1989|p=215}}</ref> [[File:Musée urbanisme de Suresnes 3.jpg|thumb|[[Suresnes-Longchamp station]] and the [[Musée d'histoire urbaine et sociale de Suresnes|museum]], inaugurated in 2013.]] In the second half of the century, as the suburbs developed and the automobile became more widely available, Suresnes became a daily stopover for many residents of western Paris, on their way to work in the capital.<ref>{{Harvtxt|Prévost|1989|p=202}}</ref> The closure of factories in the post-war period was soon accompanied by their replacement by residential estates and the headquarters of major companies. By 1989, these included the FCA advertising design company, two subsidiaries of ''[[Vivendi|Compagnie Générale des Eaux]]'' (Cable TV Teleservice) on ''Rue Diderot'', the Cegetex alarm and remote surveillance company (on the same street), the PMU computer center, [[Philips]], [[Schneider-Creusot|Schneider]], Laden, Radiola and Ignis in the Tour Gallieni, the Adélie communications and advertising agency and the [[Laboratoires Servier|Servier]] laboratory (250 researchers), L'Aérospatiale (aeronautical research, 300-strong R&D center), the ''Société Européenne de Propulsion'' (SEP, which took part in the [[Ariane (rocket family)|Ariane]] program) and [[Dassault Systèmes]]. On the corner of ''Rue Diderot'' stands the Guillaume Tell studio, in place of the former ''Le Capitole cinema'', where [[Michel Sardou]], [[Sylvie Vartan]], the [[The Rolling Stones|Rolling Stones]], [[Iron Maiden]], and [[Elton John]] recorded.<ref>{{Cite web|year=2010|title=À Suresnes, le studio-préféré des stars|language=fr|trans-title=In Suresnes, the stars’ favorite studio|url=https://www.leparisien.fr/hauts-de-seine-92/suresnes-92150/a-suresnes-le-studio-prefere-des-stars-19-08-2010-1036067.php|website=Le Parisien|access-date=September 9, 2018}}</ref> A rare vestige of Suresnes' industrial past, [[Radiotechnique]] and CFEM, which helped build the [[Eiffel Tower]], are still in existence. In October 1988, Suresnes was awarded the Marianne d'Or for economic growth.<ref>{{Harvtxt|Prévost|1989|pp=242–245}}</ref>'<ref name=":59"/> In 2005, Talend, a software company specializing in data integration, was founded in Suresnes. [[File:T2 Belvédère 2.jpg|left|thumb|The tramway at the Suresnes-Belvédère [[Île-de-France tramway Line 2|line 2]] station has replaced the Moulineaux line since 1997.]] Many festivals continue to punctuate life in Suresnes. The town follows those organized at the national level, such as the ''[[Fête de la Musique]]'' and the ''[[Bastille Day|Quatorze Juillet]]'', for example, in the fort's moat. There are also more local events, such as the ''Fête des Vendanges'', the ''Fêtes de la Seine'' (with a marine ball and a guinguette spirit), the ''Fête des Rosières'', the “''Mer à Suresnes''” for children who can't go on vacation during the summer and who are invited to take up rowing, Ciné Expo Suresnes, the ''Nuit d'automne'' ball for needy elders, or the ''Fête de l'enfance'' at the end of the school year (hosted by [[Annie Fratellini]], for example),<ref>{{Harvtxt|Prévost|1989|pp=220–226}}</ref> a ''Concours d'élégance automobile''<ref>{{Harvtxt|Prévost|1989|p=224}}</ref> (“''Suresnes Auto Rétro''”) and the ''Foulée suresnoise'', a race that attracts hundreds of participants from several European countries every year.<ref name=":58"/>
Although the number of vines has dwindled, their memory lives on, thanks to the efforts of the town council. In 1965, at a time when wine production had almost disappeared,<ref group="note">In the 1970s, a private individual named Joyeux living on ''Rue de la Huchette'' was presented as the last winemaker in Suresnes.</ref><ref name=":75"/> the town council planted white grape varieties on 70 acres of land at Pas Saint-Maurice, along the ''Boulevard du Maréchal-de-Lattre-de-Tassigny''. This clay-limestone plot had been purchased in 1926 by Henri Sellier, who had noticed the consequences of galloping urbanization and had already had a few vines planted. From 1983 onwards, planting and vinification were professionalized, and the estate grew to 1 hectare, “a sign of our commitment to preserving tradition,” says the mayor.<ref name=":11"/> As a result, Suresnes is one of the few towns in the Île-de-France region to still own a vineyard and produce wine (5,000 vines, or 4,000-5,000 bottles a year).<ref name=":4"/> Today, it's the only wine sold in the region.<ref name=":1"/> In 1984, the ''Confrérie du Taste-Vin de Suresnes'' was created by the ''Clos du Pas Saint-Maurice association'' to maintain and safeguard this heritage. Twice a year, on Saint-Vincent's Day and during the ''Fête des Vendanges'' in early October, ceremonies are held to induct new members, among whom we have counted many celebrities over the years.<ref>{{Harvtxt|Prévost|1989|p=222}}</ref><ref name=":106"/> [[File:21ème étape du Tour de France 2018, à Suresnes.jpg|thumb|21st stage of the [[2018 Tour de France, Stage 12 to Stage 21|2018 Tour de France]], in Suresnes.]] In 1990, Suresnes had a population of around 37,000.<ref name=":59"/> Former Iranian Prime Minister [[Shapour Bakhtiar]], a resident of the town, was assassinated the following year. In 1993, the annual ''Suresnes Cités Danse'' contemporary dance festival was held at the Théâtre Jean-Vilar. The [[Suresnes-Longchamp station|Suresnes-Longchamp railway station]] closed in 1993, at the same time as the line from Puteaux to Issy-Plaine (known as the Moulineaux line). It was reborn in 1997, when [[Île-de-France tramway Line 2|line 2 of the Île-de-France tramway]] was inaugurated, with two stops in the commune: Suresnes-Belvédère and [[Suresnes-Longchamp station|Suresnes-Longchamp]]. Since 2013, the former station building has housed the ''[[Musée d'histoire urbaine et sociale de Suresnes|Musée d'Histoire Urbaine et Sociale de Suresnes]]'' (Suresnes Museum of Urban and Social History), which preserves numerous objects and testimonials to the commune's past.<ref name=":62"/> In 2000, the former ''Le Capitole cinema'' (4 screens) reopened in a new building at ''3 Rue Ledru-Rollin'',<ref>{{Cite web|title=Cinéma Le Capitole à Suresnes|language=fr|trans-title=Le Capitole Cinema in Suresnes|url=https://salles-cinema.com/ile-de-france/le-capitole-suresnes|website=salles-cinema.com|access-date=September 22, 2018}}</ref> right next to the new ''Médiathèque de Suresnes'', the city's main library, which holds 130,000 documents. Another library is located in the Cité-Jardin, the ''Bibliothèque des Sorbiers'', which closes in 2019 and is being replaced by the ''Médiathèque de la Poterie'', which opens at the end of January 2020.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Nouvelle médiathèque et école d'arts plastiques|language=fr|trans-title=New media library and plastic arts school|url=https://www.suresnes.fr/projet/nouvelle-mediatheque/|website=suresnes.fr|access-date=March 6, 2020}}</ref> In 2007, a music conservatory was inaugurated on ''Place du Puits-d'Amour'', and in 2014, the Bagatelle shopping mall opened in the town center, featuring a dozen or so retailers. The following year, the city council voted to install a [[mosque]] at ''5 Rue des Velettes''. After joining the Mont-Valérien agglomeration community in 2009, in 2016 Suresnes joined the Paris Ouest La Défense territorial public establishment, as part of the [[Grand Paris|Greater Paris metropolis]]. In 2019, Suresnes will have a population of 48,620.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Présentation de la ville|language=fr|trans-title=Presentation of the city|url=https://www.suresnes.fr/ma-ville/decouvrir/presentation-de-la-ville/|website=suresnes.fr|access-date=September 8, 2018}}</ref>

{{Short description|South Korean television program}} {{Use mdy dates|date=June 2015}} {{Infobox television | image = | caption = | genre = Music | presenter = [[Shin Dong-yup (comedian)|Shin Dong-yup]] | starring = | theme_music_composer = | open_theme = | end_theme = | country = South Korea | language = Korean | director = | writer = | producer = | executive_producer = Kwon Yong-taek KBS | composer = | cinematography = | editor = | location = South Korea | camera = | runtime = 90-100 minutes | network = [[Korean Broadcasting System|KBS]] | num_seasons = | company = KBS Entertainment | first_aired = {{Start date|2011|06|04}} | last_aired = {{End date|2011|06|04}} | first_aired2 = {{Start date|2012|4|7}} | last_aired2 = present | num_episodes = 729{{efn|In addition to the regular episodes, KBS aired a special on March 3, 2018 that was not counted on the official list. Further, episodes 514, 633, 720-721 only aired in South Korea and English dubbing has not been provided, no official explanation has ever been given.}} | list_episodes = | related = | native_name = {{infobox Korean television name/auto|hangul=^불후의 명곡: 전설을 노래하다}} }}
'''''Immortal Songs: Singing the Legend''''' ({{Korean|hangul=불후의 명곡: 전설을 노래하다}}), also known as '''''Immortal Songs 2''''' ({{korean|불후의 명곡 2|labels=no}}), is a South Korean television music competition program presented by Shin Dong-yup.<ref>{{cite web|last=Park|first=Gyu-oh|title=Michael Bolton appears on 'Immortal songs 2'|url=https://kpopherald.koreaherald.com/view.php?ud=201410191432521043247_2|website=[[The Korea Herald|K-pop Herald]]|access-date=April 30, 2015|date=October 19, 2014|archive-date=March 4, 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304115553/http://kpopherald.koreaherald.com/view.php?ud=201410191432521043247_2|url-status=live}}</ref> It is a revival of ''[[Immortal Songs]]'' (2007–2009), and each episode features singers who perform their reinterpreted versions of songs.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.starnewskorea.com/stview.php?no=2011051612515339569&type=1&outlink=1|script-title=ko:아이돌 대결 '불후의명곡2', '나가수'와 차이점은?|website=[[Star News (South Korea)|Star News]]|date=May 16, 2011|access-date=November 16, 2011|language=ko|archive-date=December 17, 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131217015756/http://star.mt.co.kr/view/stview.php?no=2011051612515339569&type=1&outlink=1|url-status=live}}</ref>
The program airs a new episode every Saturday on [[KBS2]], and re-airs it with English subtitles on [[KBS World (TV channel)|KBS World]] a week or two later in the same time frame.
== Synopsis == Originally broadcast as ''Immortal Songs 2'' as a part of KBS [[Saturday Freedom]], each episode featured six [[Korean pop idol|idol singers]] who would perform songs by the singer of the episode. After restructuring in 2012, the show returned on April 7 as an independent program and rebranded as ''Immortal Songs: Singing the Legend''. Each episode now features seven singers or groups from diverse backgrounds and years of experience, ranging from members of popular K-pop idol groups to legendary solo artists. As before, they each perform their own reinterpreted versions of famous songs by the legendary singer of the episode. A noted feature of the new format is the "special episodes" which revolve around specific themes, such as festive or commemorate events.
Guest singers are seated in a waiting room with the three hosts, where they introduce themselves to viewers. The order of performances is decided randomly by presenter Shin Dong-yup drawing a ball containing the performer's name, and the 500-member audience vote after each round for their favorite performance. The performer with the most votes on the episode wins. Starting February 15, 2020, due to the COVID-19 pandemic, the votes were done by 20 special judges (mostly KBS employees). They scored the artist, but the score was not shown on screen and instead a light was used to indicate the winner.
Starting August 22, 2020, the show was re-branded as '''''Immortal Songs'''''. Moon Hee-joon & Jung Jae-hyung both left the program after being part of it for 9 & 8 years respectively and it was announced that Kim Jun-hyun and Kim Shin-young would be replacing them.<ref>{{cite news |script-title=ko:정재형·문희준 '불후의 명곡' 떠난다…15일 마지막 방송 |url=https://www.chosun.com/site/data/html_dir/2020/08/14/2020081403239.html |date=August 14, 2020 |access-date=January 10, 2021 |publisher=[[Newsis]] |via=[[The Chosun Ilbo]] |language=ko |archive-date=January 12, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210112120555/https://www.chosun.com/site/data/html_dir/2020/08/14/2020081403239.html |url-status=live }}</ref> The show's third waiting room host, Kim Tae-woo, left in June 2021 after 3 years.<ref>{{cite web |script-title=ko:'불후의 명곡' 김태우, MC석 떠난다…26일 마지막 방송 |url=https://entertain.naver.com/read?oid=421&aid=0005423952 |website=[[Naver]]|date=June 18, 2021|access-date=December 8, 2021 |archive-date=December 8, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211208030303/https://entertain.naver.com/read?oid=421&aid=0005423952 |url-status=live }}</ref> He was replaced by trot singer Shin Yu in July 2021 but he left the show in October and was replaced by a series of guest hosts (see below). Kim Shin-young also left the show in November and Lee Chan-won was named as a permanent host on November 20, 2021.
On November 4, 2021, KBS announced that it will reopen their music shows to a live audience for the first time since January 2020 when the COVID-19 pandemic began. Immortal Songs began taping episodes with a reduced live audience in late November and episode 536 was the first episode to air with an audience on December 11, 2021, with the light system remaining as the method to indicate a winner.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Ahn |first1=Tae Hyun |script-title=ko:'유스케'·'뮤뱅' 이제 직접 볼 수 있다…KBS 공개 방청 재개 |url=https://www.news1.kr/articles/?4483279 |website=News1 Korea |date=November 4, 2021 |access-date=24 October 2023 |language=Korean |archive-date=October 29, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231029152857/https://www.news1.kr/articles/?4483279 |url-status=live }}</ref>
Crossover quartet [[Forestella]] holds the unique record of most consecutive wins on the ''King of Kings'' special in the show's history, with 6 overall wins.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.spotvnews.co.kr/news/articleView.html?idxno=574831|script-title=ko:'김연아♥' 고우림 승승장구..포레스텔라, '불후' 왕중왕전 '6번째' 우승 대기록|website=Spotvnews|date=December 25, 2022|access-date=2023-03-25|language=ko|archive-date=March 25, 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230325143204/https://www.spotvnews.co.kr/news/articleView.html?idxno=574831|url-status=live}}</ref> They also had 26 consecutive matches with no losses in their 1st selection round. They are also the group with more trophies in the show, with 14 victories total in the general count sharing the top three with solo singers Jung Dong-Ha and Ali.
